ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No FoodEx2 code exists specifically for chocolate éclair; A0CKW (Choux pastry) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for chocolate éclair as of 2026.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard chocolate éclair weighs approximately 65 g and represents a typical single-serve portion as sold in bakeries and cafés.

nutrient_source.title: Chocolate éclair — Singapore HPB Foodo Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Input record cr_id F11110007 is sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo with confidence 0.95 and review_status reviewed. All core per_100g values are taken from this source. Saturated fat is adapted from USDA FoodData Central entry 'Eclair, custard-filled with chocolate glaze' (FDC ID 168898) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; the USDA value of approximately 10.5 g/100g saturated fat is used as a secondary cross-reference.

gi_source.title: Éclair, chocolate-glazed, choux pastry — GI analogous estimate based on custard-filled pastry data
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI test result for chocolate éclair is listed in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI value of 40 (low) is estimated from the closest analogous tested food: custard-filled choux pastry / cream puff, which has a reported GI of approximately 38–42 in peer-reviewed literature (Foster-Powell et al., 2002, Am J Clin Nutr 76:5–56). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (40 × 17.2) / 100 = 6.9, where carbohydrate per 65 g serving = 26.5 × 0.65 = 17.2 g.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar (14.1 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.7 g/100g), high saturated fat (~10.5 g/100g), and moderate sodium (291 mg/100g)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit chocolate éclairs due to the combination of high sugar, high saturated fat, and moderate sodium, all of which independently contribute to elevated blood pressure and arterial stiffness; if consumed, restrict to an occasional very small portion and compensate with a low-sodium, high-fibre meal.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar (14.1 g/100g), low dietary fibre (1.7 g/100g), and refined choux pastry base promoting rapid postprandial glucose rise
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid chocolate éclairs because the refined choux pastry, chocolate cream filling, and chocolate glaze together deliver significant rapidly absorbed sugar with minimal fibre buffering, causing sharp postprandial glucose excursions; if consumed at all, limit to a very small portion paired with a protein source and monitor blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (42.9 g/100g), high saturated fat (~10.5 g/100g), estimated trans fat (0.1 g/100g), and high energy density (516 kcal/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid chocolate éclairs due to the very high total fat, elevated saturated fat from butter and cream, and the presence of trans fat from pastry preparation, all of which raise LDL cholesterol and increase atherosclerotic risk; consumption should be limited to rare, very small portions within an otherwise heart-healthy dietary pattern.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High saturated fat (~10.5 g/100g from butter and cream) and estimated trans fat (0.1 g/100g), both pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: People with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid chocolate éclairs because the high saturated fat content from butter and heavy cream, combined with estimated trans fat from pastry preparation, promotes systemic inflammation and may exacerbate joint symptoms; high refined sugar further stimulates pro-inflammatory cytokine production.

llm_training.natural_language_description: A chocolate éclair is a classic French choux pastry dessert consisting of an oblong shell of light, hollow pâte à choux dough that is baked until crisp and golden, then filled with a rich chocolate-flavoured cream or custard filling and finished with a smooth chocolate glaze on top. The pastry shell is made from butter, water, flour, and eggs, producing a light yet slightly crisp exterior that contrasts with the dense, sweet, and creamy interior filling. The chocolate glaze adds a glossy, slightly firm coating with an intense cocoa flavour. Per 100 g, a chocolate éclair provides approximately 516 kcal, 42.9 g fat, 6.3 g protein, and 26.5 g carbohydrate including 14.1 g sugar and only 1.7 g dietary fibre, making it a high-energy, high-fat, high-sugar confection with limited nutritional benefit. The sodium content of 291 mg/100g is moderate. Clinically, the chocolate éclair is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its combination of high saturated fat, high sugar, low fibre, and estimated trans fat. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 40 (low category) based on analogous custard-filled choux pastry data, but the glycemic load per standard 65 g serving is approximately 6.9, which remains clinically relevant in the context of overall dietary sugar load. Chocolate éclairs are widely available in French patisseries, international bakeries, and cafés globally, and are commonly consumed as a dessert or indulgent snack.

confidence_reason: Core per_100g nutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database with a reported confidence of 0.95 and review_status of reviewed. Saturated fat is cross-referenced from USDA FoodData Central for the closest analogous entry (FDC ID 168898). GI is an analogous estimate from peer-reviewed literature (Foster-Powell et al., 2002) based on custard-filled choux pastry, which is the closest tested food type available in approved GI sources.
